title: Skeleton Job
prompt: Why do we need skeleton besides support? Choose best.

options: Protect organs like brain heart lungs and help move and make blood cells

explanation: Skeleton supports body shape, protects vital organs inside ribcage skull spine pelvis, helps movement with muscles, makes blood cells in bone marrow.
funFact: Adult human has 206 bones but baby born with about 270 that fuse together!

title: Muscles Pull
prompt: What do muscles do to move bones?

options: Pull bones they cannot push only pull

explanation: Muscles contract pulling bones at joints. They work in pairs one contracts while other relaxes.
funFact: It takes 17 muscles to smile and 43 to frown!

title: Food Groups Energy
prompt: Which food group gives us main energy to play and learn?

options: Carbohydrates like bread rice pasta potatoes

explanation: Carbohydrates provide energy. Protein builds growth repair. Fats store energy insulate. Vitamins minerals keep healthy. Fibre helps digestion. Water hydrates.
funFact: One slice bread gives energy to run about 400 metres!

title: Balanced Plate

options: Right amounts from all food groups each day

explanation: Balanced means variety proportion not too much fat sugar salt plenty fruit vegetables.
funFact: Eat rainbow colours of fruit veg to get range vitamins!

title: Protein Builders
prompt: Which foods are high in protein for growth repair muscles?

options: Chicken beans lentils eggs fish meat nuts

explanation: Protein builds body tissues muscles skin hair. Found in meat fish eggs dairy beans lentils nuts.
funFact: Spider silk is made of protein stronger than steel same thickness!

title: Calcium Strong
prompt: Which nutrient helps build strong bones and teeth?
explanation: Calcium plus vitamin D from sunlight helps bones teeth strong. Lack causes weak bones rickets.
funFact: 99 percent of body's calcium stored in bones and teeth!

title: Vertebrate Check
prompt: What do vertebrates have that invertebrates lack?
explanation: Vertebrates mammals birds fish reptiles amphibians have backbone. Invertebrates insects worms jellyfish octopus do not.
funFact: Blue whale largest vertebrate ever lived heart size small car!

title: Herbivore Carnivore Omnivore
prompt: Human eats plants and animals so human is...
explanation: Herbivore plant eater cow rabbit. Carnivore meat eater lion tiger. Omnivore both human bear pig.
funFact: Pandas are bears order Carnivora but eat almost only bamboo herbivore diet funny!

title: Muscle Pairs
prompt: When biceps contracts to bend arm what does triceps do?
explanation: Muscles work antagonistic pairs. Biceps contracts bends elbow triceps relaxes. To straighten opposite.
funFact: You have over 600 muscles in body about 40 percent body weight!

title: Water Why
prompt: Why is water important in diet besides not being food group?

options: Transports nutrients regulates temperature removes waste hydrates

explanation: Water makes up about 60 percent body weight vital for every cell function transport temperature waste.
funFact: You could survive weeks without food but only days without water!
